testingRequirements: Tests must validate user-facing behavior, not implementation details
Focus on what users actually do - integration tests over unit tests
Don't mock excessively - test real behavior where possible
Coverage metrics are meaningless - user flow coverage is everything

userStories.description:
# Port LetterGlitch Component to TypeScript ## Full Epic Plan (reference for all tickets) # LetterGlitch Loading Screen ## Context Brain Dump currently has plain text loading states ("Loading projects...", "Loading dashboard...") and no route transition animations. The goal is to use the [LetterGlitch](https://www.reactbits.dev/backgrounds/letter-glitch) canvas animation as the loading/transition screen — both for route transitions and inline loading states. No text overlay, just the pure glitch animation. ## Files to Create ### 1. `src/components/LetterGlitch.tsx` — The core component - Port the [react-bits LetterGlitch.jsx](https://github.com/DavidHDev/react-bits) to TypeScript - Props: `glitchColors`, `glitchSpeed`, `centerVignette`, `outerVignette`, `smooth`, `characters`, `className` - Canvas-based animation using `requestAnimationFrame` ### 2. `src/components/LoadingScreen.tsx` — Theme-aware wrapper - **Full-screen mode** (default): absolute fill, used for route transitions - **Compact mode** (`compact` prop): fixed height (200px), used for inline loading states - Reads `--accent-primary`, `--accent-secondary`, `--accent-ai` CSS variables at mount to build `glitchColors` array dynamically (adapts to all 9 themes) - Respects `prefers-reduced-motion`: renders a simple centered spinner (existing `Loader2` from lucide-react) instead of the canvas animation - Uses `outerVignette={true}` for the atmospheric edge fade - Custom character set: `"01█▓▒░BRAINDUMP{}[]<>/*#@&"` ## Files to Modify ### 3. `src/routes/__root.tsx` — Add router-level pendingComponent - Import `LoadingScreen` - Add `pendingComponent: () => ` to the root route config - Add `pendingMs: 300` (don't show for instant navigations) - Add `pendingMinMs: 500` (avoid flash for near-instant loads) ### 4. `src/routes/index.tsx` — Replace inline loading - Replace the `if (loading)` block's simple `

Loading projects...

` with `` ### 5. `src/routes/dashboard.tsx` — Replace inline loading - Replace the `if (loading)` block's simple `

Loading dashboard...

` with `` ### 6. `src/routes/board.tsx` — Replace inline loading - Replace the `if (loading)` block's simple `

Loading tickets...

` with `` ### 7. `src/styles.css` — Add reduced-motion rule for loading screen - Add rule to disable LetterGlitch canvas animation under `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` (the component handles this internally, but CSS class can hide the canvas as a safety net) ## Approach Details **Theme color extraction** (in LoadingScreen.tsx): ```ts const getThemeGlitchColors = (): string[] => { const style = getComputedStyle(document.documentElement); return [ style.getPropertyValue('--accent-primary').trim(), style.getPropertyValue('--accent-secondary').trim(), style.getPropertyValue('--accent-ai').trim(), ].filter(Boolean); }; ``` **Reduced motion** (in LoadingScreen.tsx): ```ts const prefersReducedMotion = window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches; ``` If true, render a simple flex-centered `` instead of the canvas. **Route transition z-index**: Use `z-index: 40` (below modals at 50+) so the loading screen doesn't cover open modals. ## Verification 1. `pnpm type-check` — no errors 2. `pnpm lint` — no errors 3. `pnpm build` — builds successfully 4. Manual testing: - Navigate between routes — full-screen glitch appears for ~500ms - Switch themes in Settings — glitch colors match the active theme - Enable "Reduce motion" in OS — falls back to simple spinner - Check board/dashboard/home pages load with compact glitch inline --- ## This Ticket's Scope Create `src/components/LetterGlitch.tsx`: - Port the react-bits LetterGlitch.jsx to TypeScript with proper types - Add typed props interface (`LetterGlitchProps`) - Canvas-based animation using `requestAnimationFrame` with proper cleanup on unmount - Support HiDPI/Retina displays via `devicePixelRatio` - Debounced resize handling ### Source Reference The original component is from https://github.com/DavidHDev/react-bits (MIT license), file: `src/content/Backgrounds/LetterGlitch/LetterGlitch.jsx` ### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Component renders a canvas with randomized glitch characters - [ ] Props are fully typed with TypeScript interface - [ ] Animation cleans up on unmount (cancelAnimationFrame, remove resize listener) - [ ] Vignette overlays work (outerVignette, centerVignette) - [ ] Smooth color transitions work when `smooth={true}` - [ ] `pnpm type-check` passes ### Branch All work for this epic goes on branch `feature/letter-glitch-loading` — shared across all tickets in the epic, single PR.

userStories.description:
# Create Theme-Aware LoadingScreen Wrapper

## Context
This is part of the LetterGlitch Loading Screen epic. All tickets share branch `feature/letter-glitch-loading` and will be part of a single PR.

## Scope

Create `src/components/LoadingScreen.tsx` — a wrapper around `LetterGlitch.tsx` that:

### Full-screen mode (default)
- Absolute positioned, fills parent container
- Used as TanStack Router `pendingComponent` for route transitions
- z-index: 40 (below modals)

### Compact mode (`compact` prop)
- Fixed height (~200px), fills width
- Used to replace inline "Loading..." text in routes

### Theme integration
- Reads CSS variables at mount: `--accent-primary`, `--accent-secondary`, `--accent-ai`
- Builds `glitchColors` array dynamically so it adapts to all 9 themes
- Uses `getComputedStyle(document.documentElement)` to extract colors

```ts
const getThemeGlitchColors = (): string[] => {
  const style = getComputedStyle(document.documentElement);
  return [
    style.getPropertyValue('--accent-primary').trim(),
    style.getPropertyValue('--accent-secondary').trim(),
    style.getPropertyValue('--accent-ai').trim(),
  ].filter(Boolean);
};
```

### Reduced motion
- Check `window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches`
- If true: render simple centered `<Loader2 className="animate-spin" />` from lucide-react
- If false: render full LetterGlitch canvas animation

### Configuration
- `outerVignette={true}` for atmospheric edge fade
- Custom character set: `"01█▓▒░BRAINDUMP{}[]<>/*#@&"`
- `glitchSpeed={50}` (default)
- `smooth={true}` for smooth color transitions

### Props Interface
```ts
interface LoadingScreenProps {
  compact?: boolean;
  className?: string;
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Full-screen mode fills container with proper z-index
- [ ] Compact mode renders at fixed height
- [ ] Colors adapt when theme is changed in Settings
- [ ] Reduced motion fallback shows spinner instead of canvas
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` passes

### Branch
All work on `feature/letter-glitch-loading` — single PR for the whole epic.

userStories.description:
# Add Route Transition pendingComponent

## Context
This is part of the LetterGlitch Loading Screen epic. All tickets share branch `feature/letter-glitch-loading` and will be part of a single PR.

## Scope

Modify `src/routes/__root.tsx` to add the LoadingScreen as TanStack Router's pendingComponent:

```ts
import { LoadingScreen } from "../components/LoadingScreen";

export const Route = createRootRoute({
  // ... existing config ...
  pendingComponent: () => <LoadingScreen />,
  pendingMs: 300,    // Don't show for instant navigations
  pendingMinMs: 500, // Minimum display to avoid flash
});
```

### Key Details
- `pendingMs: 300` — only show loading screen if navigation takes longer than 300ms
- `pendingMinMs: 500` — once shown, keep it visible for at least 500ms to avoid jarring flash
- LoadingScreen renders in full-screen mode (no `compact` prop) for route transitions
- z-index is 40 (below modals at 50+) so it doesn't interfere with open modals

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Route transitions show LetterGlitch animation when navigation takes >300ms
- [ ] Fast navigations (<300ms) don't flash the loading screen
- [ ] Loading screen doesn't cover modals
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` and `pnpm build` pass

### Branch
All work on `feature/letter-glitch-loading` — single PR for the whole epic.

userStories.description:
# Replace Inline Loading States ## Context This is part of the LetterGlitch Loading Screen epic. All tickets share branch `feature/letter-glitch-loading` and will be part of a single PR. ## Scope Replace simple text-based loading states in 3 route files with ``: ### 1. `src/routes/index.tsx` Replace the `if (loading)` block that renders `

Loading projects...

` with `` ### 2. `src/routes/dashboard.tsx` Replace the `if (loading)` block that renders `

Loading dashboard...

` with `` ### 3. `src/routes/board.tsx` Replace the `if (loading)` block that renders `

Loading tickets...

` with `` ### Also: `src/styles.css` Add a CSS safety-net rule for reduced motion: ```css @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .letter-glitch-canvas { display: none; } } ``` (The component handles this internally, but belt-and-suspenders for accessibility.) ### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Home page shows compact glitch animation while loading projects - [ ] Dashboard page shows compact glitch animation while loading - [ ] Board page shows compact glitch animation while loading tickets - [ ] Reduced motion OS setting shows spinner fallback in all locations - [ ] `pnpm type-check`, `pnpm lint`, `pnpm build` all pass - [ ] Manual verification in browser at localhost:4242 ### Branch All work on `feature/letter-glitch-loading` — single PR for the whole epic.

techStack: Database: SQLite with better-sqlite3, Drizzle ORM
State: TanStack Query for server state

dos: Use Drizzle ORM: `db.select().from(tickets)`
Use typed schema imports: `import { tickets } from "../lib/schema"`
Use `eq()`, `and()`, `sql` from drizzle-orm for conditions
Use transactions for multi-table operations: `db.transaction(() => {...})`
Use `.get()` for single row, `.all()` for multiple
donts: Raw SQL strings: `db.run("SELECT * FROM tickets")`
String-based table names
String concatenation for WHERE clauses
Multiple independent queries that should be atomic
Assume query returns what you expect without checking

dos: Use `useQuery` with `queryKeys` for data fetching
Use `useMutation` with `onSuccess` for state changes
Invalidate queries after mutations: `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: queryKeys.tickets })`
Use centralized `queryKeys` object from `src/lib/hooks.ts`
Create custom hooks in `src/lib/hooks.ts` for reusable query logic
donts: `useState` + `useEffect` for fetched data
Direct API calls without proper cache invalidation
Manual cache manipulation or refetch after every change
Hardcoded query key strings throughout components
Duplicate query setup in multiple components

dos: Use Tailwind classes for static styles: `className="p-4 bg-slate-800"`
Use CSS variables for theming: `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--shadow-modal)`
Reserve inline styles for dynamic/computed values: `style={{ width: `${percent}%` }}`
Define shared style constants at module level for referential stability
Use `React.CSSProperties` type for inline style objects when needed
donts: Inline style objects for single-use styles
Hardcoded colors that don't adapt to themes
Mix Tailwind and inline styles inconsistently
Create new objects inside components that are reused
Untyped style objects that miss IDE autocomplete

dos: Use `createServerFn({ method: "GET" })` for reads, `"POST"` for writes
Use `.inputValidator()` for type-safe input handling
Return typed data directly: `return db.select().from(tickets).all()`
Use `ensureExists()` for required lookups: `ensureExists(project, "Project")`
Import from `@tanstack/react-start` (not `@tanstack/react-start/server`)
donts: Mix GET/POST inconsistently
Access raw input without validation
Wrap in unnecessary response objects
Return null and let caller handle missing data
Wrong import path

dos: Use Zod schemas for input validation: `{ ticketId: z.string() }`
Return structured `{ content: [{ type: "text", text: ... }] }` format
Set `isError: true` for error responses
Use `log.info()` / `log.error()` from `../lib/logging.js`
Include helpful error messages: `"Project not found. Use list_projects to see available."`
donts: Trust input without validation
Return plain strings
Return error text without the error flag
`console.log` in MCP server code
Generic "Not found" errors

dos: Test user behavior: what users see, click, and experience
Integration tests for workflows: test components together
Real database fixtures with actual schema
Tests that fail when user-facing behavior breaks
Ask: "Does this test catch bugs users would encounter?"
donts: Test implementation details, internal state, or private methods
Unit tests for every function
Excessive mocking of internals
Tests that break on refactoring internals
Chase 100% code coverage as a goal

verificationSteps: Run `pnpm type-check` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm lint` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm test` - all tests must pass
Added tests for new functionality (ONLY tests that verify real user behavior - see Testing Philosophy above)
Used typed error classes (not generic `Error`)
Used Drizzle ORM (not raw SQL) - see DO/DON'T table above
Followed existing patterns from DO/DON'T tables
No hardcoded values that should be configurable
Existing tests still pass
No regressions in related functionality
Updated tests if behavior changed
Did not break backward compatibility (unless explicitly requested)
Manually verified in browser at `localhost:4242`
Checked responsive layout
Verified TanStack Query invalidates and updates correctly
Accessibility: keyboard navigation works, proper ARIA labels
Migration file created via `pnpm db:generate`
Migration tested with `pnpm db:migrate`
Backup tested if schema changed (use `pnpm brain-dump backup` then test restore)
Updated `src/lib/schema.ts` with proper types and constraints
Tested tool via Claude Code integration
Verified error responses are informative (see DO/DON'T table)
Updated tool documentation if interface changed
Added Zod schema for input validation
All acceptance criteria from ticket met
Work summary added via `comment` tool, `action: "add"` (for Ralph sessions)
Session completed with appropriate outcome (for Ralph sessions)
Committed with proper message format: `feat(<ticket-id>): <description>`

epicDescription: Replace plain text loading states and add route transition animations using a LetterGlitch canvas effect. Theme-aware, accessible, and applied both as TanStack Router pendingComponent and inline loading replacements.\n\nAll tickets in this epic share a single PR branch: `feature/letter-glitch-loading`
